creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
When running to a remote system with ssh, fairly quickly the interface resets. In many cases, it never recovers. It restarts, associates with the AP, but resets again during DHCP config. This repeats continuously. After a few attempts, the interface does not even associate. This continues and periodically associates without getting an address. Given enough time, it seems to associate in most cases, though I have let it try for over 30 minutes on one occasion.

I have no other available WiFi, so I can't rule out a hardware problem. Problem first noted about a month ago.

No other network use causes this reset.

There was a reference to this same problem in a forum thread almost a year ago. It is in the thread: 
https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/cheap-wireless-usb-adapter-rtl8188eu-rtl8821au-or-something-else.78563/

This was off-topic and was not further discussed. I suppose that this may be a hardware problem.

FreeBSD ptavv 14.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 14.0-CURRENT #4 main-n259888-701b36961cbd-dirty: Thu Dec 29 22:08:49 PST 2022     root@ptavv:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/GENERIC amd64

Running on an Alder Lake i5-1235U with the Alder Lake work-around.
